proof fn phi_1_create_key_namespace_diverges(req: CreateRequest)
    requires
        req.obj.metadata.namespace is Some,
        req.namespace != req.obj.metadata.namespace->0,
    ensures
        req.key().namespace != req.obj.metadata.namespace->0,
{

reason: CreateRequest::key uses self.namespace instead of self.obj.metadata.namespace, so the key can silently disagree with the object's own namespace metadata

proof fn phi_3_update_key_ignores_obj_name(req: UpdateRequest)
    requires
        req.obj.metadata.name is Some,

name: marshal_unmarshal_no_roundtrip
code:
proof fn phi_4_marshal_unmarshal_no_roundtrip(v: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        v.metadata.name is Some,
        v.metadata.namespace is Some,
    ensures
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal(v.marshal()) is Ok ==>
            VReplicaSetView::unmarshal(v.marshal())->Ok_0.spec() == v.spec()
            && VReplicaSetView::unmarshal(v.marshal())->Ok_0.metadata() == v.metadata(),
{
}
reason: marshal_spec/unmarshal_spec are uninterpreted with no roundtrip axiom — the solver can satisfy this vacuously by making unmarshal always fail, meaning no actual roundtrip guarantee exists

proof fn phi_5_reconcile_id_allocator_no_uniqueness(a1: ReconcileIdAllocator, a2: ReconcileIdAllocator)

{
}
reason: two independent ReconcileIdAllocators with the same counter produce identical reconcile IDs — there is no global uniqueness mechanism, so concurrent controllers can allocate colliding IDs
